rulings.Case Title: ***TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*** IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ALBERTA W. CHANDLER FBO BRUCE CHANDLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Bruce Chandlers Demurrers To The First Amended Petition For: (1) Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; (2) Conspiracy To Breach Fiduciary Duty; (3) Fraudulent Concealment; (4) Conspiracy To Defraud By Means Of Fraudulent Concealment; (5) Negligence (6) Declaratory Relief; (7) Declaratory Relief; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Bruce Chandler's Demurrer to Petitioners' First Amended Petition is sustained with leave to amend as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action. Within 20 days of the order, Petitioners must file an amended petition. Bruce Chandler's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for declaratory relief because an actual controversy does not exist regarding payment of attorney's fees and costs from the Trust. (C.C.P. Section 1060.) Petitioners seek an advisory opinion from the Court regarding the "extent to which respondents can fund their attorney fees and costs from this lawsuit by using funds from the Trust that would otherwise be paid to petitioners." (First Amended Petition, Paragraph 67.) A mere contention by a party that fees and costs are anticipated to be paid from Trust assets (regarding which no details are included in the First Amended Petition) does not create a justiciable case or controversy as required by C.C.P. Section 1060. See also Wilson v. Transit Authority (1962) 199 Cal.App.2d 716, 722 ("A difference of opinion does not give rise to a justiciable case until an actual concrete controversy arises.") The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than 10/20/2015, at 4:00 p.m.

rulings.Case Title: ***TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY*** IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ALBERTA W. CHANDLER FBO BRUCE CHANDLER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Thomas Werdels Demurrer To The First Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: Thomas Werdel's Demurrer to Petitioners Patrick and Philip Chandler's First Amended Petition (as it pertains to Werdel in his individual capacity) is sustained with leave to amend as to the second, third, and fourth causes of action. Werdel's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for declaratory relief because an actual controversy does not exist regarding payment of attorney's fees and costs from the Trust. (C.C.P. Section 1060.) Petitioners seek an advisory opinion from the Court regarding the "extent to which respondents can fund their attorney fees and costs from this lawsuit by using funds from the Trust that would otherwise be paid to petitioners." (First Amended Petition, Paragraph 67.) A mere contention by a party that fees and costs are anticipated to be paid from Trust assets does not create a justiciable case or controversy required by C.C.P. Section 1060. See also Wilson v. Transit Authority (1962) 199 Cal.App.2d 716, 722 ("A difference of opinion does not give rise to a justiciable case until an actual concrete controversy arises.") Petitioners must file an amended petition no later than 20 days from the date of the order. The prevailing party shall prepare the proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than October 20, 2015, at 4:00 p.m.
